item_description: Faint dirt marks are visible across the study floor, leading toward the safe. They appear to have been partially cleaned, but not completely removed.
The safe shows no signs of forced entry. It appears to have been opened normally, suggesting the person responsible had access or knew how to operate it.
A pair of garden shears has been set down just outside the side entrance, as if someone placed them aside before going inside.
The side entrance door was left slightly ajar. It is usually kept closed to prevent outside debris from being brought into the house.
A few small leaves are caught near the edge of the rug just outside the door to the study. They do not match any of the plants kept inside the house.
The safe shows no signs of forced entry. It appears to have been opened normally, suggesting the person responsible knew how to access it.
A sheet of paper on Victor’s desk has been torn unevenly, as if a small section was removed. The remaining notes do not seem immediately important.
A small, crumpled piece of paper is tucked partially under a table. It looks like it was stepped on or kicked aside. A short sequence of numbers is still visible.
One of the coats on the rack appears to have been handled recently, slightly out of place compared to the others. The surrounding area is otherwise neat.
The study door was left slightly ajar. It is typically kept closed when not in use.
A cleared spot on Victor’s desk suggests something was recently kept there. The surface around it has been dusted, but the space itself shows a faint outline.
The safe remains locked and undisturbed. There are no signs it has been opened recently.
A small mark is visible along the edge of the desk and on the floor below, as if something struck it and fell.
In the kitchen trash there are a few small, irregular fragments that appear to be part of something finely crafted. The material looks too valuable to have been thrown away carelessly.
A feather duster has been left in the hallway outside the study, rather than returned to its usual storage. It appears to have been set down in a hurry.
A cleared spot on Victor’s desk suggests something was recently kept there. The surrounding surface has been dusted, but the space itself shows a faint outline.
A small mark is visible along the edge of the desk and on the floor below, as if something struck it and fell.
A small, broken piece of a polished nail lies near the desk. It appears to have snapped off on impact.
One of Claire’s drawers has been left slightly open. Inside, something has been hastily tucked away beneath folded fabric.
A laptop is plugged into the wall charging. Some one must be home from work today
A cleared space on Victor’s desk suggests something was recently kept there, though there are no signs it was damaged or removed in haste.
Several items on the desk have been subtly rearranged, as if someone moved things briefly and did not return them to their exact original positions.
A decorative item sits slightly out of place, as though it had been set down temporarily and forgotten.
A drawer or storage space contains items that appear to have been put away without much care. Something about the arrangement suggests it was done without much thought.
A large vase in the living somehow rolled onto its side without breaking. It sits on the large wooden coffee table.
The safe is open, but otherwise undisturbed. There are no signs of forced entry.
One of the serving trays has been set down unevenly, as if it was placed in a hurry before being abandoned.
A decorative plant vase sits on the kitchen counter, out of place compared to the rest of the room. It looks like it was set down quickly rather than arranged with the other items.
The front door remains unlocked which is highly unusual for this household. Rarely anyone of the family or staff enter this way.
On the kitchen counter sits a note from the maid that reads 'I had a family emergency and had to leave for the day'
The glass left near where Kessler collapsed still contains a small amount of liquid. There is a faint, unusual bitterness beneath the expected scent of the drink.

Kessler’s jacket has been left in the main hall. In one of the pockets is a folded note referencing a private financial matter involving Victor, written in a tone that suggests leverage rather than cooperation.
One of the dinner plates has been set aside and not yet cleaned. It appears normal, with no unusual residue or smell.
The wine glass is marked with a decorative wine charm. None of the other glasses from the evening appear to have one attached.
A small, nearly empty vial has been left near the drink setup, partially hidden behind other items. It has a faint, bitter scent.
Kessler’s plate has been set aside and not yet cleaned. The dish appears similar to the others, though something about it seems slightly off upon closer inspection.
The evening’s menu features a rich Italian-style meal, with several garlic-heavy dishes served throughout the courses.
The tomato sauce that was made for the pasta dish exploded and has left splatters all over the entire kitchen and on the walls. Alfred is aggressively cleaning it all up at the end of the evening.
Gordon Cutler's jacket was left in the foyer. It is usually put away in the closet.
A cufflink belonging to Alfred Sterling sits on the the table after dinner. He is always moving in and out the dining room unnoticed.
Claire had written a note to her husband and it lay under the table. It read 'I thought you had everything handled. How upset is he that you lost the money?'
A weird gray liquid was splattered near the large blender. The family had eaten steak that night so it was unusual.
An embellished expense jacket laid on a stool in the kitchen. There seems to be something tucked into the pocket.
Financial documents about a transaction between Victor and Marcus were ripped up and littered all over the floor.
Claire's engagement sits on the side table near the window. It was highly unusual for her to leave her ring anywhere but on her hand or in her bedroom.
A small skinny vial lays under the dining room table empty
A purse with a large blue planner in it sits on a stool in the kitchen and describes in detail how horrible a person Claire is.
Kessler's wine glass lays shattered on the floor near the couch where he was sitting.
Lillian Harrow's shoes and jacket appear to be missing from the entryway when the evening ends. Maybe she came through a different entrance?
An old picture of Victor and Lillian hugging at a college party lay on desk.
In the chaos of Kessler's collapse at the dinner table, he threw his water glass across the table.
Lots of papers sat on the printer of different recipes for that evening's dinner.
Lots of different spices and herbs are thrown across the counter.
A calculator was left on the kitchen counter turned on.
A pair of large brown boots sat near the entryway. It is unusual for shoes to be left out even when guests attend for dinner.
A large green lamp had been knocked over and shattered all over floor.
The dining room table cloth has been stained entirely in red wine that had been spilled sometime during dinner.
The refrigerator started making noises yesterday and the freezer started to leak fluid and it oozed across the kitchen floor.
The living room was mostly tidy besides the fact that the large plant was spilling onto the floor and all dirt on the pot was seeping onto the carpet.
A large hole in the fence of the gard appeared to have been created by the neighbors dog. Flowers were dug up.

Finleigh’s schedule for the day has been edited in her usual neat handwriting. The changes are precise but unfamiliar, and do not match any adjustments Victor or Claire recall making. In a household where her schedule is strictly controlled, changes without their knowledge are highly unusual.
The coat and shoes Finleigh was wearing earlier are missing from the entry, though the rest of the closet remains undisturbed. Nothing appears to have been taken in a hurry.
The keys to the family car have been left in the kitchen, partially hidden behind a knife block rather than returned to their usual place.
A note lay on the kitchen counter that was signed by Finleigh. It read 'I am leaving. FOREVER. - Miss Finleigh'.

Victor’s copy of Finleigh’s schedule sits on his desk. It shows no changes or additions, and does not match the version found in her room. As the schedule is typically managed through him and Claire, this version appears to be the official one.
An open suitcase on the bed. No belongings inside
Two glasses filled with water sit in the living room. One appears to be drank the other does not.
Financial documents in the study had been recently shredded and discarded in the waste bin. They had been triple shredded. The police said it will take weeks if anything to figure out what they say.
There is a ransom note in the main entry hall that reads 'If I don't get the money you owe, you'll never see her again.'
The back gate of the garden is bent and twisted out of shape. It looks like someone would need a lot of strength to be able to do this.
Bloody footprints lead out of Finleigh's room and then disappear in the hallway. The footprints are decently large and appear to be loafers or boots.
A dirty cloth lay on the kitchen counter that appeared to have a little bit of blood on it.
A lamp lay shattered on the floor in the study. The top of the lamp was missing and was not found in the house.
The main door to the house was left open and the door lock was broken as if someone had broke in.
Papers in the study were scattered everywhere. Most of them belonged to Victor's financial firm.
A large chair is overturned in the bedroom. The chair is very light. There is a droplet of blood on the carpet but otherwise the room is tidy and neat.
A suiting jacket that does not belong to any member of the family sits at the bottom of the stairs. It appears to also have specks of blood on it.
The house keys Finleigh uses where left in the tin in the main entryway. She brings them everywhere with her.
A white bloody glove in found in the trash can. The other glove is not found.
Specks of blood are splatters across the main entryway. They lead to the stairs toward the bedroom.
Cleaning To-List remains in the kitchen. Everything is checked off as completed for the day.
The living room carpet has dirt tracked all over it from outside from the gardener earlier in the morning when he waters the flowers.
Bag handle that appear to be ripped off of a duffle bag is stuck to the corner of the dresser near the door. The space seems tidy and clean otherwise.
A large swiffer and swiffer pads lay in the main hall. They are usually stored in the closet after they have been used to clean the main hall.
The windows are covered in dust and dirt that has been accumulating for some time.
A few personal items appear to be missing from Finleigh's room, though nothing obvious at first glance. The space remains tidy, but not everything has been left behind.

A section of the garden path shows light signs of recent use, as though passed through a few times. Two sets of footprints can be made out. One set of large boot prints is a common sight throughout the garden, but the other set of footprints alongside them are much smaller and lighter and not commonly seen out here.
The side gate behind the tool shed is not fully secured. It appears to have been opened recently and closed again without being properly latched.
The coat and shoes Finleigh was wearing earlier, as well as a few of her others, are gone, though the rest of the closet remains undisturbed. Nothing appears to have been taken in a hurry.

A neatly written note has been left behind in Finleigh’s room. It's written in her neat looping handwriting and in it, she states that she is leaving of her own accord and that no effort should be made to find her. The tone is composed, though the wording is more direct than expected.
